pattern: Because a segment can only ever be halved into two equal contiguous pieces, the reachable segments form the fixed shape of a balanced binary tree over the string -- so the minimum cost is a memoized recursion (or, equivalently, a build-up over that tree) comparing 'keep this segment whole' against 'split it into its two halves and add their best costs'.
hints: Precompute prefix counts of '1' so the cost of keeping any segment whole is an O(1) lookup: flatCost if it has zero sensitive characters, otherwise length times count times encCost.
A segment can only be split when its length is even, and only into two equal halves -- so recursively compare the whole-segment cost against the sum of the two halves' best costs.
Memoize on the segment's (start, end) bounds; because every split is an exact bisection, the total number of distinct segments you ever recurse into is proportional to n log n, not exponential.

edge_inputs.description: splitting all the way down to single characters is cheapest
splitting once into two halves beats going further or not splitting at all
no sensitive characters at all, single flat segment wins outright
single character, odd length, cannot split
all sensitive characters -- splitting is still worthwhile since cost scales with length times count
odd total length forces the top level segment to stay whole even though it contains sensitive characters
encCost so cheap relative to flatCost that never splitting is optimal
flatCost so cheap that keeping the all-zero segment whole is better than splitting it further
power-of-two length with sensitive characters spread evenly, forcing recursion all the way down

stress.description: random binary strings of varied length and costs, including odd lengths that block splitting at the top
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    encCost = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    flatCost = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    return [s, encCost, flatCost]

stress.description: small strings for exhaustive-style cross-checking of every possible partition tree
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    encCost = rng.randint(1, 20)
    flatCost = rng.randint(1, 20)
    return [s, encCost, flatCost]
